description:
The Certificate of Analysis documents the material (e.g. chemical compound) properties describing a material's quality or performance factors specific to manufacturing batch.
The exact material properties depend on the material type and the following manufacturing processes. They are defined bilaterally between a supplier (manufacturer) and customer.

BpnlTrait.description: The provided regular expression ensures that the BPNL is composed of prefix 'BPNL', 10 digits and two alphanumeric letters.

Timestamp.description: Describes a Property which contains the date and time with an optional timezone.

OrderIdCharacteristic.description: This is a proprietary order ID, generated by either the supplier side or customer side.

CertificateMetaDataCharacteristic.description: Defines the administrative and traceability information of the Certificate of Analysis, linking issue dates with conformity assessments and bilateral order identifiers.

issueDate.description: The date and timestamp of issuing this certificate.

isConformWithSpecification.description: In the case that any of the properties that are part of the Certificate of Analysis violates the defined upper or lower limit this property should be set to false.

supplierOrderId.description: This is the order number on the supplier side that is given by e.g., the supplier's enterprise resource planning system.

customerOrderId.description: This is the order number on the customer side that can be given by e.g., the customer's enterprise resource planning system.

Text.description: Describes a Property which contains plain text. This is intended exclusively for human readable strings, not for identifiers, measurement values, etc.

ValueCharacteristic.description: The string should be formatted in a way sp that it is easily interpretable or convertible to the xsd datatype given in the field "data type".

DataTypeEnumerationCharacteristic.description: Allows specification of any xsd data type allowed in SAMM 2.2.0 without the subtypes of xsd:integer.

UnitReference.description: Describes a Property containing a reference to one of the units in the Unit Catalog.

MethodEntity.description: A method with its authority, ID and description detailing how the material property was tested.

methodStandard.description: Reference to the standard that described the method.

methodDescription.description: This field should be used to describe the method or provide additional information to the referenced norm.

MethodSet.description: Characteristic for a set of methods on how the material property was tested.

MaterialProperty.description:
A material property with its name, definition measured value and specification (limits).
If available, lower limit and upper limit must be given, they are only optional in the case that the limits don't exist or don't apply.

description.description:
This field should be used to describe the property.

If available a reference to a public or internal norm should be provided. The norm should specify the meaning of the property as well as the specification of the analysis method used.

value.description: The result of the analysis for this specific property.

lowerLimit.description:
The lower limit of the specification with a boundary condition of "At least".
Values below that limit are considered out of specification. Values exactly at the limit are considered in specification.

upperLimit.description:
The upper limit of the specification with a boundary condition of "At most".
Values above that limit are considered out of specification. Values exactly at the limit are considered in specification.

dataType.description: The data type of the properties value and limits. The data type should specify how the string value should be type casted or interpreted.

unit.description: The unit of measure used for the property value and specification limits.

method.description: A set of methods that describe how the part was tested.

ProductTypeInformationCharacteristic.description: Specifies the material or chemical compound identification schema used across the supply chain, reconciling manufacturer and customer part naming conventions.

nameAtManufacturer.description: Name of the part as assigned by the manufacturer

manufacturerPartId.description: Part ID as assigned by the manufacturer of the part. The Part ID identifies the part (as designed) in the manufacturer`s dataspace. The Part ID does not reference a specific instance of a part and thus should not be confused with the serial number or batch number.

customerPartId.description:
Part ID as assigned by the customer of the part. The customer Part ID identifies the part (as designed)in the customer`s dataspace. The Part ID does not reference a specific instance of a part and thus should not be confused with the serial number or any other instance IDs. 
If no specific part ID exists a part family ID may be substituted for it.

AllowedKeys.description:
The key characteristic of a local identifier. A specific subset of keys is predefined. Custom keys are not allowed. Predefined keys:
- "manufacturerId" - The Business Partner Number (BPN) of the manufacturer. Value: BPN-Nummer
- "batchId" - The identifier of the batch, to which the serialized part belongs

KeyValueList.description: A list of key value pairs for local identifiers, which are composed of a key and a corresponding value.

key.description: The key of a local identifier.

LocalIdentifierCharacteristic.description: A part may have multiple attributes, which uniquely identify that part in a specific dataspace (e.g., the manufacturer's dataspace)

BpnsTrait.description: The provided regular expression ensures that the BPNS is composed of prefix 'BPNS', 10 digits and two alphanumeric letters.

FunctionCharacteristic.description: Describes the characteristics of the function for a site related to the respective part.

SitesEntity.description: Describes the ID and function of a site for the associated part in the AsBuilt context(e.g. SerialPart, Batch, jIS).

siteId.description: The identifier of the site according to BPDM. The siteId must be a valid BPN. The BPN is a unique, unchangeable identifier for Business Partners / company locations from foundation to closure, regardless of the different business relationships / structures between or within the Business Partners or company locations.

function.description: The function of the site in relation to the part(i.e. the activity within the value chain of the part that is performed at the site)

ManufacturingCharacteristic.description: Characteristic to describe manufacturing related data

productionDate.description: Timestamp of the manufacturing date as the final step in production process (e.g. final quality check, ready-for-shipment event)

country.description: Alphabetic three-character (alpha-3) code representing a country name.

sites.description: A site is a delimited geographical area where a legal entity does business(geographical address with geo coordinates).A site always has a primary physical address. It is possible that further physical addresses are specified for the site. P.O. box details are only possible in addition to the physical address. A site has a 1:n relation to addresses, means at least 1 address is necessary and multiple addresses are possible.

UuidV4Trait.description: The provided regular expression ensures that the UUID is composed of five groups of characters separated by hyphens, in the form 8-4-4-4-12 for a total of 36 characters (32 hexadecimal characters and 4 hyphens), optionally prefixed by "urn:uuid:" to make it an IRI.

ProductInstanceInformationCharacteristic.description: Captures batch-specific traceability data for the analyzed material, connecting manufacturing origin to quality assessment and shelf-life parameters.

localIdentifiers.description: A local identifier enables identification of a part in a specific dataspace, but is not unique in the Catena-X dataspace. Multiple local identifiers may exist.

manufacturingInformation.description: Information from manufacturing process, such as manufacturing date and manufacturing country

globalAssetId.description: The fully anonymous ID in the dataspace of the part.

expiryDate.description: The expiry date of the material.

DisclaimerMetadataCharacteristic.description: Contextual information about disclaimer applicability, such as automated generation notices or signature waiver statements for digital certificates.

DisclaimerRequirementCharacteristic.description: Formal statement delineating quality assurance boundaries and customer verification responsibilities regarding material fitness-for-purpose beyond analytical specifications.

DisclaimerCharacteristic.description: Structures the legal framework surrounding the Certificate of Analysis, balancing analytical accuracy documentation with contractual responsibility boundaries.

disclaimerMetadata.description:
A set of data describing the characteristics and context of the disclaimer within a document.
It includes information about its origin, purpose, language, version, scope of validity and relationship with the main document.
This metadata allows the disclaimer to be identified and managed as a traceable and reusable element within the document system.

disclaimerRequirement.description:
Criteria or specifications that define the expected quality of a product or service.
They serve as a basis for assessing conformity and ensuring that the product complies with agreed standards or technical specifications.

DocumentTypeCharacteristic.description: Characteristic for document type classification information.

statement.description: A formal declaration regarding the validity and authenticity of the document, such as whether it was automatically generated or requires a signature.

BinaryDocumentCharacteristic.description: Characteristic for a Base64-encoded binary document attachment.

payload.description: Base64-encoded string of the binary document file (e.g., PDF).

documentLanguage.description: ISO 639-1 language code of the document content.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the encoded document (e.g., application/pdf).

customer.description: The customer as a BPNL reference.

supplier.description: The supplier (manufacturer) as a BPNL reference.

materialProperties.description:
A set of material properties containing the specification and the actual material analysis measurement results.

The properties may vary based on the material type as well as the agreed specification of the material and the analysis between the customer and manufacturer (supplier).

productInstanceInformation.description: Product instance related information, in the case of Certificate of Analysis a product instance refers to a batch.

disclaimer.description:
A legal or technical statement that limits the liability of the issuer of the document and clarifies the scope of the information provided.
It usually specifies that the data is for reference only or that the user is responsible for verifying the suitability of the product.

documentType.description:
Classification that identifies the nature and purpose of a document within a system.
It defines how it should be managed, what information it contains and what processes it is involved in.

binaryDocument.description: A binary representation of the Certificate of Analysis as a Base64-encoded document (e.g., PDF). This allows embedding the original document directly within the data payload.
